LanguageData.NPCStyleLimiter_InheritingGlobal: This race is currently inheriting the Global configuration.
LanguageData.NPCStyleLimiter_HomeSubTitle: Easily customize spawning weights and restrictions for apparel, hairstyles, beards, and body types.
LanguageData.NPCStyleLimiter_HomeGuide1: Sidebar: Switch between editing the "Global Config" and "Specific Config" for individual races on the left.
LanguageData.NPCStyleLimiter_HomeGuide2: Inheritance: A "●" indicator next to a race means specific config is active. Otherwise, it inherits the Global configuration.
LanguageData.NPCStyleLimiter_HomeGuide3: Gender Separation: Enable "Configure by Gender" to customize separate styles and probabilities for male and female pawns.
LanguageData.NPCStyleLimiter_HomeGuide4: Adjusting Weights: Use the sliders to adjust spawn probabilities. 1.0x is default, up to 5.0x. Turn off the toggle or drag to 0.0x to disable a style.
LanguageData.NPCStyleLimiter_HomeGuide5: Gender Ratio: Customize the male-to-female ratio of newly spawned pawns for each race.
LanguageData.NPCStyleLimiter_HomeLimitDesc: IMPORTANT: To ensure maximum compatibility with other mods and respect vanilla faction/pawn class gear filters, all spawning chances in this mod scale the base vanilla values (Vanilla Chance × Custom Multiplier).\nTherefore, if a style (apparel, hair, beard) is naturally prohibited for a pawn by vanilla rules (has a base weight of 0 due to faction, gender, or class limitations), setting its multiplier to 5.0x will NOT make it spawn.
LanguageData.NPCStyleLimiter_HomeSafetyDesc: If you accidentally disable all valid clothing items for a race, the game's spawning engine will freeze or crash because it cannot find any apparel.\nTo prevent this, the mod features a built-in safety valve. If a pawn ends up with zero eligible clothes due to your filters, the mod will temporarily allow basic vanilla outfits to keep the game running smoothly. We recommend keeping at least a few common outfits enabled for each race.
LanguageData.NPCStyleLimiter_HomeAlienDesc: Some custom alien races (e.g., Milira) may only show a single "placeholder" hairstyle. This is because their actual hair assets are rendered directly as gene graphics or body texture additions (Addons) rather than standard game hair definitions (Def).\nSince these styles do not exist in the game's hair database and their random assignments are handled internally by their respective mods, they cannot be filtered here. To ensure compatibility, we recommend using mods like "Character Editor" to manually adjust or lock specific alien pawn cosmetics in-game.
